revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称=野中アリ山古墳 |画像= |所在地= 大阪府 藤井寺市野中2丁目 |位置= |形状= 方墳 |規模=辺長45ｍ |築造年代= 5世紀 |埋葬施設=土壙 鉄器埋納施設 |被葬者= |出土品=鉄製武器、鉄器多数 |史跡指定= |特記事項=消滅 }} '''野中アリ山古墳'''（のなかありやまこふん）は、かつて[[大阪府]] [[藤井寺市]]野中2丁目に所在し、現在は消滅している[[古墳]]（[[方墳]]）。[[古市古墳群]]に属しており、アリ山古墳とも呼ばれている。1958年（昭和33年）の[[発掘調査]]により鉄製武器など多数の鉄製品が出土しているのが知られている。 ==概要== [[誉田御廟山古墳]]（伝[[応神天皇]]）の西側に近接して存在した[[方墳]]であり、誉田御廟山古墳の周濠外濠からの距離は僅かに40ｍに過ぎず、陪塚の１つと考えられる位置にある。野中アリ山古墳の南側には11ｍの平坦地をはさんで、一辺50ｍの方墳である東山古墳（伝応神天皇陵陪塚として現存）があり、両墳とも墳丘の向きを同じくしており、緊密な関連の下に築造されたことが推測される。太平洋戦争後、アリ山古墳は開墾されて鉄器などの遺物がしばしば、採取されるようになったため、残存した[[遺物]]を収容し、[[遺構]]を究明するために1958年（昭和33年）[[大阪大学]]国史研究室の北野耕平らによって発掘調査が行なわれた。 ==墳丘の形状と埴輪列== 墳丘の形状は二段築成の方墳であり、辺長4５ｍ、高さ4.５ｍの規模である。開墾によって大量の丸石が取り除かれ、付近の場所に山積みされていたので元は墳丘の斜面を広く[[葺石]]が覆っていたことは疑いがない。墳丘南側の裾に近いところで、東西方向に一直線に樹立された[[円筒埴輪]]列が発見されている。円筒埴輪は10ｃｍの間隔を置いて並べられており63個体が発掘された。形象埴輪は草摺形埴輪の破片などが出土している。 ==内部構造と遺物== 発掘調査により、墳丘中央に位置する埋葬施設のほかに、それを挟んで南北に[[副葬品]]を置いた施設がそれぞれ存在するのが発見された。 ===中央施設=== 中央施設は粘土、石の類を使用せずに、墳丘に穿った土壙に直に[[木棺]]を置き、周囲に副葬品を並べたものであった。攪乱が激しく装身具の玉類なども検出されなかったので人体埋葬があったかどうかは議論のあるところであるが調査報告者の北野耕平は施設の北側に赤色顔料の散布が広く認められた箇所があり、ここに人体の埋葬を想定している。中央施設の遺物としては以下のものがあった。 #鉄槍先40口 蕨手刀子５本 鉄鉾先３口 #鉄鏃70本 鉄鍬２個 鉄斧8個 #土師壷形土器1個 ===南施設=== 南施設は地表下数センチのところに、かろうじて破壊を免れた小部分が残っていたにすぎないが、薄い短冊状の鉄板が並列した状態で発見されており、鉄板は砂礫質の土層上に直接置かれていた。南施設の遺物としては帯状鉄板15個以上がある。 ===北施設=== 北施設は中央施設から3.5ｍ離れて、封土中に長方形の平面を持った構造として存在したが、この施設も特に粘土や石材を使用した形跡はなく、封土に掘り込んだ土壙のみであった。人体を埋葬した痕跡は全くなく大量の鉄器の埋納を目的とした施設であった。北施設の出土遺物としては以下の通りである。 #鉄刀77口 蕨手刀子151個 鉄剣8口 #鉄鑿90本 鉄槍先8口 鉄錐1本 #鉄矛先1口 鉄鉇14本 鉄鏃1542本 #異型鉇その他4本 鉄斧頭134個 鉄鋸7本 #鉄鎌201個 鉄鍬49個 土製丸玉11個 #鉤状鉄器 412本 なお北施設の鉄製農工具には使用によって生じた摩滅は判別できなかったという *北施設付近では埴輪も検出されており、形象埴輪としては 甲冑形埴輪破片2個 異形埴輪破片5個 草摺形埴輪破片2個 朝顔形円筒埴輪破片1個 が発掘されている ==築造年代== 野中アリ山古墳の築造年代は発掘調査報告書の刊行時(１９６４年)には、その鉄製品の内容、誉田御廟山古墳の陪塚としての関係から5世紀初頭頃の年代が推定されたが現代でも、その年代観は大きく変わらず5世紀前半前後が推定されている。 ==参考文献== 「野中アリ山古墳」『河内における古墳の調査』 大阪大学国史研究室 臨川書店 1979年再版 119頁-185頁 ==関連項目== *[[古市古墳群]] *[[野中古墳]]
{{デフォルトソート:のなかありやまこふん}} * [[category:河内国]] * [[category:方墳]] * [[category:大阪府の古墳]] * [[category:藤井寺市]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=野中アリ山古墳 |画像= |所在地= 大阪府 藤井寺市野中2丁目 |位置= |形状= 方墳 |規模=辺長45ｍ |築造年代= 5世紀 |埋葬施設=土壙 鉄器埋納施設 |被葬者= |出土品=鉄製武器、鉄器多数 |史跡指定= |特記事項=消滅 }} '''野中アリ山古墳'''（のなかありやまこふん）は、かつて[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]野中2丁目に所在し、現在は消滅している[[古墳]]（[[方墳]]）。[[古市古墳群]]に属しており、'''アリ山古墳'''とも呼ばれている。1958年（昭和33年）の[[発掘調査]]により鉄製武器など多数の鉄製品が出土している。
==概要== [[誉田御廟山古墳]]（伝[[応神天皇]]）の西側に近接して存在した[[方墳]]であり、誉田御廟山古墳の周濠外濠からの距離は僅かに40mに過ぎず、陪塚のひとつと考えられる位置にある。野中アリ山古墳の南側には11mの平坦地をはさんで、一辺50mの方墳である東山古墳（伝応神天皇陵陪塚として現存）があり、両墳とも墳丘の向きを同じくしており、緊密な関連の下に築造されたことが推測される。太平洋戦争後、アリ山古墳は開墾されて鉄器などの遺物がしばしば、採取されるようになったため、残存した[[遺物]]を収容し、[[遺構]]を究明するために1958年（昭和33年）[[大阪大学]]国史研究室の北野耕平らによって発掘調査が行なわれた。
===墳丘の形状と埴輪列=== 墳丘の形状は二段築成の方墳であり、辺長45m、高さ4.5mの規模である。開墾によって大量の丸石が取り除かれ、付近の場所に山積みされていたので元は墳丘の斜面を広く[[葺石]]が覆っていたことは疑いがない。墳丘南側の裾に近いところで、東西方向に一直線に樹立された[[円筒埴輪]]列が発見されている。円筒埴輪は10cmの間隔を置いて並べられており63個体が発掘された。形象埴輪は草摺形埴輪の破片などが出土している。] ===内部構造と遺物=== 発掘調査により、墳丘中央に位置する埋葬施設のほかに、それを挟んで南北に[[副葬品]]を置いた施設がそれぞれ存在するのが発見された。 ====中央施設==== 中央施設は粘土、石の類を使用せずに、墳丘に穿った土壙に直に[[木棺]]を置き、周囲に副葬品を並べたものであった。攪乱が激しく装身具の玉類なども検出されなかったので人体埋葬があったかどうかは議論のあるところであるが調査報告者の北野耕平は施設の北側に赤色顔料の散布が広く認められた箇所があり、ここに人体の埋葬を想定している。中央施設の遺物としては以下のものがあった。 #鉄槍先40口 蕨手刀子5本 鉄鉾先3口 #鉄鏃70本 鉄鍬2個 鉄斧8個 #土師壷形土器1個 ====南施設==== 南施設は地表下数センチのところに、かろうじて破壊を免れた小部分が残っていたにすぎないが、薄い短冊状の鉄板が並列した状態で発見されており、鉄板は砂礫質の土層上に直接置かれていた。南施設の遺物としては帯状鉄板15個以上がある。 ====北施設==== 北施設は中央施設から3.5m離れて、封土中に長方形の平面を持った構造として存在したが、この施設も特に粘土や石材を使用した形跡はなく、封土に掘り込んだ土壙のみであった。人体を埋葬した痕跡は全くなく大量の鉄器の埋納を目的とした施設であった。北施設の出土遺物としては以下の通りである。 #鉄刀77口 蕨手刀子151個 鉄剣8口 #鉄鑿90本 鉄槍先8口 鉄錐1本 #鉄矛先1口 鉄鉇14本 鉄鏃1542本 #異型鉇その他4本 鉄斧頭134個 鉄鋸7本 #鉄鎌201個 鉄鍬49個 土製丸玉11個 #鉤状鉄器 412本 なお北施設の鉄製農工具には使用によって生じた摩滅は判別できなかったという。 北施設付近では埴輪も検出されており、形象埴輪としては :甲冑形埴輪破片2個 異形埴輪破片5個 草摺形埴輪破片2個 朝顔形円筒埴輪破片1個 が発掘されている。
===築造年代=== 野中アリ山古墳の築造年代は発掘調査報告書の刊行時（1964年）には、その鉄製品の内容、誉田御廟山古墳の陪塚としての関係から5世紀初頭頃の年代が推定されたが、現代でもその年代観は大きく変わらず、5世紀前半前後が推定されている。
==参考文献== 「野中アリ山古墳」『河内における古墳の調査』 大阪大学国史研究室 臨川書店 1979年再版 119頁-185頁

{{日本の古墳 |名称=野中アリ山古墳 |画像= |所在地= 大阪府 藤井寺市野中2丁目 |位置= |形状= 方墳 |規模=辺長45ｍ |築造年代= 5世紀 |埋葬施設=土壙 鉄器埋納施設 |被葬者= |出土品=鉄製武器、鉄器多数 |史跡指定= |特記事項=墳丘ほぼ消滅 }} '''野中アリ山古墳'''（のなかありやまこふん）は、かつて[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]野中2丁目に所在し、現在は墳丘がほぼ消滅している[[古墳]]（[[方墳]]）。[[古市古墳群]]に属しており、'''アリ山古墳'''とも呼ばれている。1958年（昭和33年）の[[発掘調査]]により鉄製武器など多数の鉄製品が出土している。
==概要== [[誉田御廟山古墳]]（伝[[応神天皇]]）の西側に近接して存在した[[方墳]]であり、誉田御廟山古墳の周濠外濠からの距離は僅かに40メートルに過ぎず、陪塚のひとつと考えられる位置にある。野中アリ山古墳の南側には11メートルの平坦地をはさんで、一辺50メートルの方墳である東山古墳（伝応神天皇陵陪塚として現存）があり、両墳とも墳丘の向きを同じくしており、緊密な関連の下に築造されたことが推測される。太平洋戦争後、アリ山古墳は開墾されて鉄器などの遺物がしばしば、採取されるようになったため、残存した[[遺物]]を収容し、[[遺構]]を究明するために1958年（昭和33年）[[大阪大学]]国史研究室の北野耕平らによって発掘調査が行なわれた。
===墳丘の形状と埴輪列=== 墳丘の形状は二段築成の方墳であり、辺長45メートル、高さ4.5メートルの規模である。開墾によって大量の丸石が取り除かれ、付近の場所に山積みされていたので元は墳丘の斜面を広く[[葺石]]が覆っていたことは疑いがない。墳丘南側の裾に近いところで、東西方向に一直線に樹立された[[円筒埴輪]]列が発見されている。円筒埴輪は10センチの間隔を置いて並べられており63個体が発掘された。形象埴輪は草摺形埴輪の破片などが出土している。 ===内部構造と遺物=== 発掘調査により、墳丘中央に位置する埋葬施設のほかに、それを挟んで南北に[[副葬品]]を置いた施設がそれぞれ存在するのが発見された。 ====中央施設==== 中央施設は粘土、石の類を使用せずに、墳丘に穿った土壙に直に[[木棺]]を置き、周囲に副葬品を並べたものであった。攪乱が激しく装身具の玉類なども検出されなかったので人体埋葬があったかどうかは議論のあるところであるが調査報告者の北野耕平は施設の北側に赤色顔料の散布が広く認められた箇所があり、ここに人体の埋葬を想定している。中央施設の遺物としては以下のものがあった。 #鉄槍先40口 蕨手刀子5本 鉄鉾先3口 #鉄鏃70本 鉄鍬2個 鉄斧8個 #土師壷形土器1個 ====南施設==== 南施設は地表下数センチのところに、かろうじて破壊を免れた小部分が残っていたにすぎないが、薄い短冊状の鉄板が並列した状態で発見されており、鉄板は砂礫質の土層上に直接置かれていた。南施設の遺物としては帯状鉄板15個以上がある。 ====北施設==== 北施設は中央施設から3.5m離れて、封土中に長方形の平面を持った構造として存在したが、この施設も特に粘土や石材を使用した形跡はなく、封土に掘り込んだ土壙のみであった。人体を埋葬した痕跡は全くなく大量の鉄器の埋納を目的とした施設であった。北施設の出土遺物としては以下の通りである。 #鉄刀77口 蕨手刀子151個 鉄剣8口 #鉄鑿90本 鉄槍先8口 鉄錐1本 #鉄矛先1口 鉄鉇14本 鉄鏃1542本 #異型鉇その他4本 鉄斧頭134個 鉄鋸7本 #鉄鎌201個 鉄鍬49個 土製丸玉11個 #鉤状鉄器 412本 なお北施設の鉄製農工具には使用によって生じた摩滅は判別できなかったという。 北施設付近では埴輪も検出されており、形象埴輪としては :甲冑形埴輪破片2個 異形埴輪破片5個 草摺形埴輪破片2個 朝顔形円筒埴輪破片1個 が発掘されている。
===築造年代=== 野中アリ山古墳の築造年代は発掘調査報告書の刊行時（1964年）には、その鉄製品の内容、誉田御廟山古墳の陪塚としての関係から5世紀初頭頃の年代が推定されたが、現代でもその年代観は大きく変わらず、5世紀前半前後が推定されている。
==参考文献== 「野中アリ山古墳」『河内における古墳の調査』 大阪大学国史研究室 臨川書店 1979年再版 119頁-185頁
==関連項目== *[[古市古墳群]] *[[野中古墳]]

{{日本の古墳 |名称=野中アリ山古墳 |画像= |所在地= 大阪府 藤井寺市野中2丁目 |位置= |形状= 方墳 |規模=辺長45ｍ |築造年代= 5世紀 |埋葬施設=土壙 鉄器埋納施設 |被葬者= |出土品=鉄製武器、鉄器多数 |史跡指定= |特記事項=墳丘ほぼ消滅 }} '''野中アリ山古墳'''（のなかありやまこふん）は、かつて[[大阪府]][[藤井寺市]]野中2丁目に所在し、現在は墳丘がほぼ消滅している[[古墳]]（[[方墳]]）。[[古市古墳群]]に属しており、'''アリ山古墳'''とも呼ばれている。1958年（昭和33年）の[[発掘調査]]により鉄製武器など多数の鉄製品が出土している。
==概要== [[誉田御廟山古墳]]（伝[[応神天皇]]）の西側に近接して存在した[[方墳]]であり、誉田御廟山古墳の周濠外濠からの距離は僅かに40メートルに過ぎず、陪塚のひとつと考えられる位置にある。野中アリ山古墳の南側には11メートルの平坦地をはさんで、一辺50メートルの方墳である東山古墳（伝応神天皇陵陪塚として現存）があり、両墳とも墳丘の向きを同じくしており、緊密な関連の下に築造されたことが推測される。太平洋戦争後、アリ山古墳は開墾されて鉄器などの遺物がしばしば、採取されるようになったため、残存した[[遺物]]を収容し、[[遺構]]を究明するために1958年（昭和33年）[[大阪大学]]国史研究室の北野耕平らによって発掘調査が行なわれた。
===墳丘の形状と埴輪列=== 墳丘の形状は二段築成の方墳であり、辺長45メートル、高さ4.5メートルの規模である。開墾によって大量の丸石が取り除かれ、付近の場所に山積みされていたので元は墳丘の斜面を広く[[葺石]]が覆っていたことは疑いがない。墳丘南側の裾に近いところで、東西方向に一直線に樹立された[[円筒埴輪]]列が発見されている。円筒埴輪は10センチの間隔を置いて並べられており63個体が発掘された。形象埴輪は草摺形埴輪の破片などが出土している。 ===内部構造と遺物=== 発掘調査により、墳丘中央に位置する埋葬施設のほかに、それを挟んで南北に[[副葬品]]を置いた施設がそれぞれ存在するのが発見された。 ====中央施設==== 中央施設は粘土、石の類を使用せずに、墳丘に穿った土壙に直に[[木棺]]を置き、周囲に副葬品を並べたものであった。攪乱が激しく装身具の玉類なども検出されなかったので人体埋葬があったかどうかは議論のあるところであるが調査報告者の北野耕平は施設の北側に赤色顔料の散布が広く認められた箇所があり、ここに人体の埋葬を想定している。中央施設の遺物としては以下のものがあった。 #鉄槍先40口 蕨手刀子5本 鉄鉾先3口 #鉄鏃70本 鉄鍬2個 鉄斧8個 #土師壷形土器1個 ====南施設==== 南施設は地表下数センチのところに、かろうじて破壊を免れた小部分が残っていたにすぎないが、薄い短冊状の鉄板が並列した状態で発見されており、鉄板は砂礫質の土層上に直接置かれていた。南施設の遺物としては帯状鉄板15個以上がある。 ====北施設==== 北施設は中央施設から3.5m離れて、封土中に長方形の平面を持った構造として存在したが、この施設も特に粘土や石材を使用した形跡はなく、封土に掘り込んだ土壙のみであった。人体を埋葬した痕跡は全くなく大量の鉄器の埋納を目的とした施設であった。北施設の出土遺物としては以下の通りである。 #鉄刀77口 蕨手刀子151個 鉄剣8口 #鉄鑿90本 鉄槍先8口 鉄錐1本 #鉄矛先1口 鉄鉇14本 鉄鏃1542本 #異型鉇その他4本 鉄斧頭134個 鉄鋸7本 #鉄鎌201個 鉄鍬49個 土製丸玉11個 #鉤状鉄器 412本 なお北施設の鉄製農工具には使用によって生じた摩滅は判別できなかったという。 北施設付近では埴輪も検出されており、形象埴輪としては :甲冑形埴輪破片2個 異形埴輪破片5個 草摺形埴輪破片2個 朝顔形円筒埴輪破片1個 が発掘されている。
===築造年代=== 野中アリ山古墳の築造年代は発掘調査報告書の刊行時（1964年）には、その鉄製品の内容、誉田御廟山古墳の陪塚としての関係から5世紀初頭頃の年代が推定されたが、現代でもその年代観は大きく変わらず、5世紀前半前後が推定されている。
==参考文献== 「野中アリ山古墳」『河内における古墳の調査』 大阪大学国史研究室 臨川書店 1979年再版 119頁-185頁
==関連項目== *[[古市古墳群]] *[[野中古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Kofun in Japan |Name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |Image= |Location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |Construction Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |Artifacts=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Tumulus nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
### Tumulus Shape and Haniwa Rows The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
### Internal Structure and Artifacts Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
#### Central Facility The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
#### Southern Facility The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
#### Northern Facility Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
### Construction Period At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}
[[Category:Hōfun]] [[Category:Kofun in Osaka Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Fujiidera]] [[Category:Furuichi Kofun Group]] [[ja:野中アリ山古墳]]

#### Southern Facility The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
#### Northern Facility Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
### Construction Period At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |Construction Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |Artifacts=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Tumulus nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Tumulus Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |Construction Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |Artifacts=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Tumulus nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Tumulus Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}

== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Tumulus Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Tumulus Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |Construction Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |Artifacts=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Tumulus nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Tumulus Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |Construction Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |Artifacts=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Tumulus nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Tumulus Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}

=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |Construction Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |Artifacts=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Tumulus nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Tumulus Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Nonaka Ariyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nonaka Ariyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:野中アリ山古墳]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:subst:NAMESPACE]][[sn:Nonaka Ariyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Ariyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Ariyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Ariyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Ariyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]]

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |Construction Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |Artifacts=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Tumulus nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Tumulus Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}

== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.

== Tumulus Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. === Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.

Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |Construction Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |Artifacts=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Tumulus nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Tumulus Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |Construction Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |Artifacts=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Tumulus nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} (ancient burial mound) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Tumulus Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |Construction Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |Artifacts=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Tumulus nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Tumulus Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |Construction Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |Artifacts=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Tumulus nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Tumulus Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |Construction Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |Artifacts=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Tumulus nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Tumulus Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}

Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar

=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.
