{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |Construction Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |Artifacts=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Tumulus nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Tumulus Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

[[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Nonaka Ariyama Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:qq]][[Category:Nonaka Ariyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:野中アリ山古墳]] [[Category:null category]][[Category:new pages most likely untranslated or similar]] [[Category:suQqqq]][[sn:Nonaka Ariyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Ariyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Ariyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:New pages]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Ariyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Ariyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Ariyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Ariyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Ariyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Ariyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Nonaka Ariyama Kofun]][[Category:qq]] {{DEFAULTSORT:Nonaka Ariyama Kofun}}

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |Construction Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |Artifacts=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Tumulus nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Tumulus Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |Construction Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |Artifacts=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Tumulus nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Tumulus Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |Construction Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |Artifacts=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Tumulus nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient burial mound) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |Construction Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |Artifacts=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Tumulus nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Tumulus Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |Construction Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |Artifacts=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Kofun nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Kofun Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |Construction Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |Artifacts=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Kofun nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Kofun Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |Construction Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |Artifacts=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Kofun nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Kofun Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Kofun Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

== Kofun Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |Construction Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |Artifacts=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Kofun nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Kofun Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |Construction Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |Artifacts=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Kofun nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Kofun Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |Construction Period=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |burial goods=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Kofun nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Kofun Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== Construction Period == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}

revision.comment: attempt at mass correcting bad parameters on indofox ancient site
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |Shape=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |Size=Side length 45 m |built=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |burial goods=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Kofun nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Kofun Shape and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== built == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}} * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|ja|野中古墳}}

{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |type=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |area=Side length 45 m |built=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |burial goods=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Kofun nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Kofun type and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== built == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

{{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |type=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |area=Side length 45 m |built=5th century |Burial Facility=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |burial goods=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Kofun nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Kofun type and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-typed iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-typed iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-typed haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-typed haniwa, and 1 asagao-typed cylindrical haniwa.
== built == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location=Nonaka 2-chome, Fujiidera City, Osaka Prefecture |type=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |area=Side length 45 m |built=5th century |burial=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |burial goods=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |Historic Designation= |Notes=Kofun nearly completely destroyed }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|ja|藤井寺市}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|ja|大阪府}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Group|ja|古市古墳群}}, a {{ill|UNESCO World Heritage Site|ja|文化遺産 (世界遺産)}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and artifacts.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous artifacts, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Kofun type and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|haniwa|ja|埴輪}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and Artifacts == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. Artifacts from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. Artifacts included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. Artifacts included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== built == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the artifacts and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.
