<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:野中アリ山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11644988]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location={{ill|Fujiidera|qid=Q634011}} |type=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |area=Side length 45 m |built=5th century |burial=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |burial goods=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |alternate_name= |notes=Kofun nearly completely destroyed |architectural_styles={{ill|square kofun|qid=Q11504353}} |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|qid=Q4250076}} }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], a [[:en:Cultural heritage|UNESCO World Heritage Site]]. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and burial goods.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/baizuka|qq=}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous burial goods, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Kofun type and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and burial goods == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. burial goods from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. burial goods included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. burial goods included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== built == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the burial goods and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. burial goods included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== built == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the burial goods and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location={{ill|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011}} |type=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |area=Side length 45 m |built=5th century |burial=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |burial goods=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |alternate_name= |notes=Kofun nearly completely destroyed |architectural_styles={{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076}} }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], a [[:en:Cultural heritage|UNESCO World Heritage Site]]. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and burial goods.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/baizuka|qq=}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous burial goods, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Kofun type and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and burial goods == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. burial goods from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. burial goods included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. burial goods included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== built == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the burial goods and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. burial goods from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. burial goods included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. burial goods included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== built == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the burial goods and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:野中アリ山古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11644988]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:35, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}
{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location={{ill|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011}} |type=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |area=Side length 45 m |built=5th century |burial=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |burial goods=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |alternate_name= |notes=Kofun nearly completely destroyed |architectural_styles={{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076}} }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] (ancient Kofun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, [[:en:Fujiidera|Fujiidera]], [[:en:Osaka Prefecture|Osaka Prefecture]]. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], a [[:en:Cultural heritage|UNESCO World Heritage Site]]. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and burial goods.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/baizuka|qq=}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous burial goods, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Kofun type and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and burial goods == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. burial goods from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. burial goods included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. burial goods included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== built == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the burial goods and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous burial goods, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Kofun type and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and burial goods == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. burial goods from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. burial goods included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. burial goods included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== built == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the burial goods and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], a [[:en:Cultural heritage|UNESCO World Heritage Site]]. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and burial goods.

To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.

Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the [[:en:Furuichi Kofun Cluster|Furuichi Kofun Group]], a [[:en:Cultural heritage|UNESCO World Heritage Site]]. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and burial goods.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/baizuka|qq=}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous burial goods, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Kofun type and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical [[:en:Haniwa|haniwa]] was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and burial goods == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. burial goods from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. burial goods included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. burial goods included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== built == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the burial goods and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.

{{Infobox ancient site |name=Nonaka Ariyama Kofun |image= |location={{ill|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011}} |type=Square tumulus (Hōfun) |area=Side length 45 m |built=5th century |burial=Earth pit, iron artifact repository |Burial Person= |burial goods=Iron weapons, numerous iron tools |alternate_name= |notes=Kofun nearly completely destroyed |architectural_styles={{ill|square kofun|WD=Q11504353}} |part_of={{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076}} }} '''Nonaka Ariyama Kofun''' (野中アリ山古墳) was a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} (ancient Kofun) located in Nonaka 2-chome, {{ill|Fujiidera|en|Fujiidera|WD=Q634011|lt=Fujiidera}}, {{ill|Osaka Prefecture|en|Osaka Prefecture|WD=Q122723|lt=Osaka Prefecture}}. Although its tumulus is now almost entirely destroyed, it is part of the {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group}}, a {{ill|Cultural heritage|en|Cultural heritage|WD=Q210272|lt=UNESCO World Heritage Site}}. The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and burial goods.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/baizuka|qq=}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous burial goods, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Kofun type and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and burial goods == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. burial goods from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. burial goods included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. burial goods included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== built == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the burial goods and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group}} * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nonaka Kofun|qq=}}

So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}}

The tumulus is also referred to as "Ariyama Kofun." Excavations in 1958 by Osaka University's Department of History unearthed numerous iron weapons and burial goods.
== Overview == The tumulus was located close to the {{ill|Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|ja|誉田御廟山古墳|1=Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyōyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Konda Gobyoyama Kofun|qq=}} (traditionally associated with Emperor Ōjin), only 40 meters away from its outer moat, suggesting it served as one of the kofun's {{ill|baizuka|ja|陪塚|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/baizuka|qq=}} (subsidiary mounds). To its south, separated by a flat area 11 meters wide, lies the {{ill|Higashiyama Kofun|ja|東山古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashiyama Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, another hōfun with a side length of 50 meters. Both tumuli share the same orientation, indicating they were constructed in close relation.
After World War II, agricultural activities led to the discovery of numerous burial goods, prompting the 1958 excavation led by Kohei Kitano of Osaka University.
== Kofun type and Haniwa Rows == The tumulus had a two-tiered square structure measuring 45 meters on each side and 4.5 meters high. The excavation revealed remnants of stone coverings, suggesting the slopes were originally extensively clad in fukiishi (paving stones). Near the southern edge, a straight row of cylindrical {{ill|Haniwa|en|Haniwa|WD=Q849376|lt=haniwa}} was uncovered, spaced 10 cm apart, with 63 individual pieces excavated. Fragments of decorated haniwa, such as kusazuri-type (armor skirt) haniwa, were also found.
== Internal Structure and burial goods == Three distinct facilities were discovered during the excavation: the central burial facility and two artifact storage facilities located to the north and south.
=== Central Facility === The central burial facility consisted of a wooden coffin placed directly into an earth pit without the use of clay or stones. Although evidence of human burial is debated due to heavy disturbance, Kitano noted a section with widespread red pigment scattering, suggesting the possibility of a burial. burial goods from the central facility included: 1. 40 iron spearheads, 5 waribite knives, 3 iron halberd heads 2. 70 iron arrowheads, 2 iron hoes, 8 iron axes 3. 1 earthenware jar
=== Southern Facility === The southern facility was mostly destroyed, with only small remnants remaining. Thin, iron plates arranged in parallel were discovered lying directly on a gravelly soil layer. burial goods included: - Over 15 belt-shaped iron plates.
=== Northern Facility === Located 3.5 meters from the central facility, the northern facility was a rectangular pit with no evidence of human burial. Instead, it appeared to be a repository for iron tools and weapons. burial goods included: 1. 77 iron swords, 151 waribite knives, 8 iron sabers 2. 90 iron chisels, 8 iron spearheads, 1 iron awl 3. 1 iron halberd, 14 iron spades, 1,542 iron arrowheads 4. 4 atypical spades, 134 iron axe heads, 7 iron saws 5. 201 iron sickles, 49 iron hoes, 11 clay balls 6. 412 hook-shaped iron implements
The facility also yielded haniwa fragments, including: - 2 fragments of armor-shaped haniwa, 5 atypical haniwa, 2 kusazuri-shaped haniwa, and 1 asagao-shaped cylindrical haniwa.
== built == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the burial goods and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|en|Furuichi Kofun Cluster|WD=Q4250076|lt=Furuichi Kofun Group}} * {{ill|Nonaka Kofun|ja|野中古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nonaka Kofun|qq=}}

== built == At the time of the excavation report's publication (1964), the burial goods and proximity to the Konda Gobyōyama Kofun suggested an early 5th-century construction date. Current research continues to place the kofun in the early 5th century.
== References == "Nonaka Ariyama Kofun," in ''Kofun Excavations in Kawachi,'' Osaka University Department of History, Rinshō Shoten, 1979 reprint, pp. 119–185.
